title: Advanced Dungeons & Dragons

jokes.text: Opening narrator describing Neil as 'Fat Neil' in epic fantasy voice
jokes.setup: Epic fantasy narrator voice establishing a hero's journey
jokes.punchline: The 'hero' is immediately identified by his cruel nickname
jokes.explanation: Strong opening that subverts fantasy tropes by centering on a character defined by bullying. The juxtaposition of epic narration with cruel nickname is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Jeff the Liar, son of William the Barely Known
jokes.setup: Fantasy naming convention for heroic characters
jokes.punchline: Jeff's epithet reveals his dishonesty and daddy issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - perfectly captures Jeff's defining traits in fantasy language. Economy is strong with multiple layers of meaning.

jokes.text: Pierce the Insensitive, known also as Pierce the Dickish and Grandpa the Flatulent
jokes.setup: Fantasy naming convention continues
jokes.punchline: Pierce gets three increasingly crude epithets
jokes.explanation: Perfect character distillation with escalating crudeness. The three names capture Pierce's essence while building comedically.

jokes.text: At the end of the meeting, they realized that Chang had been there and felt too awkward to mention it
jokes.setup: Group forms secret plan to help Neil
jokes.punchline: Chang was silently present the whole time and everyone was too uncomfortable to acknowledge it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy that feels uniquely Community. The casual mention of social awkwardness elevated to absurd levels is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: So we're just gonna ignore that hate crime, huh?
jokes.setup: Group acts casual about their D&D plan around Neil
jokes.punchline: Troy directly calls out the 'Fat Neil' nickname as a hate crime
jokes.explanation: Sharp, direct line that cuts through everyone's politeness. Perfect economy - maximum impact in minimal words. Classic Troy observation.

jokes.text: I'm an elf, not a nerd
jokes.setup: Shirley suggests Troy should wear armor
jokes.punchline: Troy distinguishes between being an elf and being a nerd while playing D&D
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Troy trying to maintain coolness while fully engaged in nerd activity. Character-appropriate denial.

jokes.text: It's cool, and when you play it, it makes you happy... Like a dragon
jokes.setup: Neil questions why they suddenly want to play D&D
jokes.punchline: Annie's awkward attempt to sound knowledgeable about D&D
jokes.explanation: Annie's transparent attempt to sound D&D-savvy while clearly knowing nothing. The 'like a dragon' comparison is perfectly awkward for her character.

jokes.text: Shouldn't there be a board or some pieces or something to Jenga?
jokes.setup: Abed explains this is a role-playing game in imagination
jokes.punchline: Jeff expects physical game components and references wrong game
jokes.explanation: Jeff's confusion about role-playing games shows his outsider status. The Jenga reference adds to his cluelessness.

jokes.text: I am a dwarf named Zippity-Do. Okay, I'm not the best at making up names
jokes.setup: Shirley introduces her character
jokes.punchline: She immediately acknowledges her terrible naming ability
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor where Shirley acknowledges her limitation immediately. The name 'Zippity-Do' is appropriately terrible.

jokes.text: Boy, you weren't kidding
jokes.setup: Shirley just admitted she's bad at names
jokes.punchline: Troy's response to Jeff's equally terrible name
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that highlights the pattern of bad character names. Troy's direct observation works as a reaction beat.

jokes.text: My name is Bing Bong the Archer. I'm an archer and such
jokes.setup: Everyone's making terrible character names
jokes.punchline: Shirley doubles down with an even sillier name and redundant description
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Shirley's terrible naming ability. 'Bing Bong' is appropriately childish, and 'and such' shows her discomfort with the game.

jokes.text: I'm...Ew! Hector the well-endowed? Abed
jokes.setup: Britta picks up a character sheet randomly
jokes.punchline: She gets an inappropriate character name clearly meant for Troy
jokes.explanation: Britta's discomfort with the sexual character name creates good cringe comedy. Sets up Abed's response about Troy.

jokes.text: I didn't know you'd just grab one at random. I made that one with Troy in mind
jokes.setup: Britta got an inappropriate character name
jokes.punchline: Abed reveals he specifically designed sexual character for Troy
jokes.explanation: Abed's matter-of-fact delivery of inappropriate character design makes it funnier. Good character moment showing his directness.

jokes.text: Yeah, I bet you did
jokes.setup: Abed admits making sexual character for Troy
jokes.punchline: Troy's uncomfortable acknowledgment of the implication
jokes.explanation: Troy's dry response to the awkward situation. Simple but effective reaction that doesn't overplay the moment.

jokes.text: Worst introduction ever. Check it. I am Brutalitops, the magician! Magic user, baby... What?
jokes.setup: Troy criticizes Annie's long introduction
jokes.punchline: His 'better' introduction is equally awkward and he gets immediately attacked
jokes.explanation: Troy's confidence immediately undercut by his own awkwardness and then game consequences. 'Brutalitops' is a great ridiculous name.

jokes.text: I want to know why these goblins are attacking us. Maybe these woods are their rightful land, and from their perspective...
jokes.setup: Group is being attacked by goblins
jokes.punchline: Britta tries to understand the political motivations of imaginary monsters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - bringing social justice perspective to fantasy combat. Excellent character integration showing her need to politicize everything.

jokes.text: You're the AT&T of people
jokes.setup: Britta is over-analyzing the goblin attack
jokes.punchline: Jeff delivers cutting corporate metaphor for being terrible
jokes.explanation: One of the episode's best lines. Perfectly captures Jeff's wit and disdain for Britta. The corporate metaphor is original and economical - maximum insult in minimal words.

jokes.text: 'Magic user, baby... What?' - Troy's confusion about his own character
jokes.setup: Troy confidently calls himself a magician
jokes.punchline: He's immediately confused when Abed corrects him to 'magic user'
jokes.explanation: Troy's overconfidence immediately deflated by not understanding game terminology. His confused reaction is perfect.

jokes.text: What am I not good at? Sex.
jokes.setup: Jeff boasts about his knife-throwing success
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately deflates him with sexual inadequacy joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Troy's immediate, brutal response. Maximum comedic impact with minimal words. Classic roast-style comeback.

jokes.text: Dial it back, Lavernica
jokes.setup: Annie over-enthusiastically thanks Neil for saving them
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaks character to tell Annie to tone down the obvious ego-boosting
jokes.explanation: Jeff acknowledging the transparent nature of their confidence-building exercise while staying in character. Good meta-awareness without breaking the scene.

jokes.text: First of all, gay. Second of all, stupid. And thirdly...
jokes.setup: Pierce discovers the D&D game
jokes.punchline: His immediate, crude dismissal in numbered format
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce bigotry in his typical enumerated format. Character-appropriate but relies on offensive stereotypes rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: But you remembered to invite Al Jolson here
jokes.setup: Pierce complains about being excluded
jokes.punchline: Racist reference to Chang as blackface performer
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate racism that establishes Pierce as the villain, but relies on offensive references rather than clever writing. More plot function than comedy.

jokes.text: And you remembered to let fatty sit in my chair. Get out! You're stretching it!
jokes.setup: Pierce continues complaining about exclusion
jokes.punchline: He cruelly calls Neil fat and accuses him of stretching his chair
jokes.explanation: Establishes Pierce's cruelty toward Neil but relies on fat-shaming rather than clever writing. More plot setup than genuine comedy.

jokes.text: As you watch the goblins retreat, you notice a 67-year-old naked man with no weapons, lying in the wet grass, shivering. His name is... Pierce Hawthorne, and I'm 66, dick.
jokes.setup: Abed needs to incorporate Pierce into the game
jokes.punchline: Pierce's pathetic introduction and immediate age correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Pierce's pathetic game entrance. His vanity about age correction adds character depth to the humiliation.

jokes.text: I wait 14 turns
jokes.setup: Abed says Pierce will die of exposure in 13 turns
jokes.punchline: Jeff coldly chooses to let Pierce die
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Jeff moment - calculated cruelty delivered with maximum economy. The precision of '14 turns' shows his vindictive intelligence.

jokes.text: I attack him! I attack Black Face!
jokes.setup: Pierce gets Neil's sword and continues being aggressive
jokes.punchline: Pierce attacks Chang with racist epithet
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier racist comment but still relies on offensive content rather than clever writing. More about establishing Pierce as irredeemable than generating laughs.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll wipe my ass with it and throw it off a cliff. That's for sitting in my chair, fatty
jokes.setup: Neil pleads for his sword that took 10 years and 50 campaigns to get
jokes.punchline: Pierce threatens disgusting desecration and reveals petty motivation
jokes.explanation: Pierce's escalating cruelty and petty motivation revealed. More plot development than comedy - establishing his villainy through gross-out threats.

jokes.text: I can't hear you over the sound of me rubbing his sword on my balls
jokes.setup: Group pleads with Pierce to return the sword
jokes.punchline: Pierce describes disgusting act of defiling Neil's prized possession
jokes.explanation: Pierce's juvenile gross-out humor as psychological warfare. Character-consistent but relies on shock value rather than wit.

jokes.text: You have... Successfully rubbed your balls on the sword
jokes.setup: Pierce describes crude act with the sword
jokes.punchline: Abed delivers game mechanics update with deadpan professionalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - treating disgusting act as just another game mechanic. His deadpan delivery and commitment to DM role makes gross humor sophisticated.

jokes.text: I'm sick of you threatening me and talking to me like a kid and giving me that look you give me like I can't get erections!
jokes.setup: Pierce rants about how Jeff treats him
jokes.punchline: His complaints escalate to bizarre sexual insecurity
jokes.explanation: Pierce's escalating complaints peak with unexpected sexual insecurity. The specificity of 'that look you give me' makes it more personal and funnier.

jokes.text: If that's sarcasm, I can't tell, because everything in this game is silly
jokes.setup: Abed mentions Pegasus in response to travel question
jokes.punchline: Jeff acknowledges the absurdity of the fantasy setting
jokes.explanation: Jeff's meta-commentary on fantasy tropes while trapped within them. Good self-awareness without breaking the scene completely.

jokes.text: And so it was that the group began to describe themselves walking. And as they described themselves walking, so did Abed confirm that they walked... I walk with them. Until...
jokes.setup: Narrative describing the mechanics of role-playing travel
jokes.punchline: Chang awkwardly inserting himself into the narration
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about RPG mechanics with Chang's awkward insertion. The repetitive structure mimics fantasy prose while highlighting the artificiality.

jokes.text: Argy bargy arg barg barg. Why is he a pirate? He's a gnome. He only speaks gnome
jokes.setup: Group meets gnome waiter who speaks in gibberish
jokes.punchline: Troy assumes pirate, Abed explains it's just gnome language
jokes.explanation: Troy's logical but wrong assumption about fantasy speech patterns. The gibberish 'argy bargy' is appropriately silly for D&D parody.

jokes.text: You are a human warrior, which is five species classes greater than I. That's disgusting. Don't talk like that
jokes.setup: Gnome waiter shows extreme deference to Annie's character
jokes.punchline: Annie is uncomfortable with fantasy racism hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Annie's real-world social justice sensibilities clash with fantasy game mechanics. Good character consistency and commentary on D&D's problematic elements.

jokes.text: Okay, here's all the Dungeons & Dragons stuff I could find. I had to carry it all the way from my car. It's very heavy, and my doctor said...
jokes.setup: Neil arrives with D&D materials
jokes.punchline: He's struggling with heavy boxes and starts explaining medical restrictions
jokes.explanation: Neil's over-helpfulness and tendency to share medical information. Character-consistent but relatively weak physically-based humor.

jokes.text: Excuse me, I don't normally do this, but you're the most beautiful creature I've ever seen, and I would really love a Pegasus
jokes.setup: Jeff needs to flirt with elf maiden for Pegasus
jokes.punchline: Abed delivers Jeff's 'seduction' as transactional pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of Jeff's pickup style - combining fake sincerity with direct request for what he wants. Abed's delivery makes Jeff's superficiality clear.

jokes.text: My stable has a bedding of heather. Perhaps you'd be more comfortable indoors. Abed, try to look at what you're doing and understand that I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Elf maiden flirts back with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaks character to protest the uncomfortable roleplay
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy as Jeff realizes he's trapped in romantic roleplay. His direct appeal to Abed breaks the fourth wall effectively.

jokes.text: I take her by the hand and lead her to the stable. I light a candle and rip off a piece of my tunic, which I fashion into a blindfold
jokes.setup: Troy takes over the romantic encounter
jokes.punchline: He immediately escalates to kinky sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Troy's unexpected sexual confidence in roleplay versus real life. The detailed scenario shows his fantasy investment.

jokes.text: How long is that? Cool, cool, cool.
jokes.setup: Troy asks about appropriate spooning duration after fantasy sex
jokes.punchline: Abed's signature nervous phrase shows his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Abed's signature phrase used to deflect from uncomfortable sexual content. Character-consistent nervous response to Troy's detailed fantasy.

jokes.text: 'So many things, Abed... So many things' - Pierce's ominous response about his plans
jokes.setup: Abed asks Pierce what he wants to do next in the game
jokes.punchline: Pierce's vaguely threatening response suggests elaborate revenge
jokes.explanation: Good building of tension. Pierce's mysterious tone suggests something big is coming.

jokes.text: My name... was Kyle. Kyle. Britta? He was an imaginary waiter. I wouldn't expect you to understand
jokes.setup: Dying gnome reveals his name was Kyle
jokes.punchline: Neil gets emotional while Britta dismisses imaginary character death
jokes.explanation: Neil's emotional investment in imaginary characters contrasted with Britta's rational dismissal. Shows how deeply Neil needs this fantasy world.

jokes.text: Tell me, Abed, are you familiar with the Amburg region of the Edylmann River basin? Of course I am.
jokes.setup: Pierce asks about obscure fantasy geography
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately knows the made-up location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his encyclopedic knowledge extends to fictional geography. Sets up Pierce's trap while showing Abed's D&D mastery.

jokes.text: Scream what I told you to scream! Pierce Hawthorne is my master!
jokes.setup: Pierce controls the dragon and demands proclamation
jokes.punchline: Dragon must declare Pierce as master like a sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Pierce's power fantasy reaching ridiculous heights. The demand for verbal submission shows his need for dominance and control.

jokes.text: This is why I wanted to play Chutes and Ladders
jokes.setup: Pierce has ultimate power and is tormenting everyone
jokes.punchline: Shirley wishes they'd played simpler, less dramatic game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley response - wanting to avoid conflict and complexity. Chutes and Ladders as preferred alternative to emotional devastation is very her.

jokes.text: Make him as fat as Fat Neil. Oh, right, cry. Let it out. Baste your chubby cheeks in tears of gravy
jokes.setup: Pierce transforms Neil's character to be fat
jokes.punchline: His cruel taunting escalates to food metaphors
jokes.explanation: Pierce's cruelty reaching its peak with the 'tears of gravy' line. More about establishing his villainy than generating laughs through clever writing.

jokes.text: Look, before you respond, I can make it up to you. I'll find a fatter Neil
jokes.setup: Jeff is caught creating the 'Fat Neil' nickname
jokes.punchline: His solution is to find someone fatter to redirect the cruelty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff response - trying to solve cruelty with more cruelty through logical redistribution. Shows his complete moral blindness in brilliant, horrible way.

jokes.text: So call him Skinny Neil. He's actually not that skinny. He's bald! He's black!
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests calling other Neil 'Skinny Neil'
jokes.punchline: Jeff lists other distinguishing features with increasing inappropriateness
jokes.explanation: Jeff's escalating inappropriate suggestions for nickname alternatives. Shows his casual prejudice while trying to appear progressive.

jokes.text: Well, I don't look at the world through that lens
jokes.setup: Jeff just listed racial characteristics as distinguishing features
jokes.punchline: He claims to be colorblind immediately after being obviously not
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment where Jeff claims colorblindness seconds after racial categorization. Perfect satire of performative progressive language.

jokes.text: For my turn, I rape the Duquesne family... Again
jokes.setup: Neil shows Pierce pity instead of fear
jokes.punchline: Pierce escalates to horrific threats, revealing this is recurring behavior
jokes.explanation: Pierce's desperation to maintain power through shock value. The 'again' reveals pattern of using sexual violence as intimidation - more disturbing than funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, you really outdid yourself this week. There's a lot of pain in you. I seriously feel bad
jokes.setup: Group continues showing pity for Pierce
jokes.punchline: They recognize his behavior stems from genuine pain
jokes.explanation: Troy and Neil's mature recognition of Pierce's underlying pain. Shows character growth and emotional intelligence that defuses Pierce's power.

jokes.text: Oh, sweet baby Jesus
jokes.setup: Neil needs to roll 18 or higher for difficult shot
jokes.punchline: His exclamation suggests he rolled exactly what he needed
jokes.explanation: Neil's religious exclamation at his miraculous dice roll. Perfect setup for his heroic moment and character redemption through luck and skill.

jokes.text: The dragon eating Pierce after being freed
jokes.setup: Neil's sword destroys the amulet controlling the dragon
jokes.punchline: The freed dragon immediately turns on Pierce and eats him
jokes.explanation: Perfect karmic justice. Pierce's attempt to control power turns against him immediately. Classic fantasy trope executed well.

jokes.text: That was the best game I ever played in my life. Don't mention it. Want to play again next week? Maybe
jokes.setup: Neil expresses gratitude for the game experience
jokes.punchline: His tentative response suggests renewed confidence and options
jokes.explanation: Perfect character arc resolution. Neil's 'maybe' shows he now has confidence and choices, no longer desperately needing their friendship.

jokes.text: And so it was that Pierce Hawthorne saved the life of Fat Neil, while learning very, very little
jokes.setup: Epic narrator returns to conclude the story
jokes.punchline: Pierce gets credit for saving Neil despite being the antagonist, and learned nothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic conclusion that Pierce inadvertently saved Neil by being so terrible he united everyone against him. 'Very, very little' is perfect Pierce assessment.

jokes.text: If you had a tail, people could always tell when you're happy. Yes, but with giant ears, you could hear things from miles away, and you could wear backpacks as earrings
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed debate body modification preferences
jokes.punchline: Abed's practical considerations for giant ears include fashion applications
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy and Abed weird conversation. Abed's logical approach to hypothetical body modifications with unexpected fashion consideration is perfectly him.

jokes.text: Backpacks already free up your hands
jokes.setup: Abed says backpack earrings would free up hands
jokes.punchline: Troy points out backpacks already do that
jokes.explanation: Troy's logical puncturing of Abed's elaborate reasoning. Simple but effective observation that deflates Abed's overcomplicated justification.

tmdb_overview: Abed invites "Fat Neil" to play a game of Dungeons & Dragons with the rest of the study group, hoping to boost his confidence, after Jeff becomes concerned about him being a loner. When Pierce finds out he was not invited, he joins anyway, disrupting their plans.
insight: D&D obsession escalates into sustained cringe comedy across 56 tightly-wound jokes.
